quarantine.mr_name: CIGAR canonicalization preserves alignment semantics
quarantine.oracle: The read name, flag, RNAME, POS, MAPQ, RNEXT, PNEXT, TLEN, SEQ, and all optional tags remain identical after canonicalizing CIGAR match operators (= vs M). The alignment position and mate information are unchanged.

evidence.quote: CIGAR string uses M for alignment match/mismatch; = and X are equivalent to M for alignment position purposes. Canonicalizing = to M (or M to =) does not change the alignment semantics.

quarantine.mr_name: CIGAR zero-length operation removal preserves alignment
quarantine.oracle: The read name, flag, RNAME, POS, MAPQ, RNEXT, PNEXT, TLEN, SEQ, and all optional tags remain identical. The alignment position and all semantic fields are unchanged.

evidence.quote: Zero-length CIGAR operations (e.g., 0M) have no effect on alignment and can be removed without changing the alignment semantics.

quarantine.mr_name: CIGAR split/merge adjacent ops preserves alignment
quarantine.oracle: The read name, flag, RNAME, POS, MAPQ, RNEXT, PNEXT, TLEN, SEQ, and all optional tags remain identical. The alignment position is unchanged.

evidence.quote: Adjacent CIGAR operations of the same type (e.g., 3M4M) can be merged (7M) or split without changing alignment semantics.

quarantine.mr_name: SEQ case normalization preserves record semantics
quarantine.oracle: The read name, flag, RNAME, POS, MAPQ, CIGAR, RNEXT, PNEXT, TLEN, and all optional tags remain identical. Only the case of SEQ bases changes.

evidence.quote: SEQ bases can be represented in upper or lower case; the case does not affect alignment semantics.

quarantine.mr_name: RNEXT canonicalization preserves mate information
quarantine.oracle: The read name, flag, RNAME, POS, MAPQ, CIGAR, PNEXT, TLEN, SEQ, and all optional tags remain identical. RNEXT value '=' is equivalent to RNAME and the transformation preserves mate pairing semantics.

evidence.quote: In SAM format, RNEXT field can use '=' to denote the same reference as RNAME. Converting between '=' and the explicit reference name preserves mate information.

quarantine.mr_name: Optional tag permutation preserves record content
quarantine.oracle: The read name, flag, RNAME, POS, MAPQ, CIGAR, RNEXT, PNEXT, TLEN, and SEQ remain identical. All optional tags are preserved with the same key-value pairs, only their order changes.

evidence.quote: Optional tags in SAM records have no defined order; reordering them does not change the record semantics.

quarantine.mr_name: Header record reordering preserves SAM semantics
quarantine.oracle: All header records and their tag values are preserved. The order of header record types (HD, SQ, RG, PG, etc.) and records within each type does not affect the interpretation of the SAM file.

evidence.quote: The SAM specification does not mandate a specific order for header records; reordering them preserves the semantics.

quarantine.mr_name: HD subtag shuffling preserves header version info
quarantine.oracle: All HD subtags (VN, SO, etc.) and their values are preserved. The order of subtags within the HD line does not affect the interpretation.

evidence.quote: The order of key-value pairs within a SAM header line is not semantically significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SQ record subtag shuffling preserves reference info
quarantine.oracle: All SQ subtags (SN, LN, etc.) and their values are preserved. The order of subtags within each SQ line does not affect the interpretation.

evidence.quote: The order of key-value pairs within a SAM SQ header line is not semantically significant.

quarantine.mr_name: RG record subtag shuffling preserves read group info
quarantine.oracle: All RG subtags (ID, SM, LB, etc.) and their values are preserved. The order of subtags within each RG line does not affect the interpretation.

evidence.quote: The order of key-value pairs within a SAM RG header line is not semantically significant.

quarantine.mr_name: PG record subtag shuffling preserves program info
quarantine.oracle: All PG subtags (ID, PN, CL, PP, VN, etc.) and their values are preserved. The order of subtags within each PG line does not affect the interpretation.

evidence.quote: The order of key-value pairs within a SAM PG header line is not semantically significant.

quarantine.mr_name: CO comment shuffling preserves header comments
quarantine.oracle: All CO (comment) lines are preserved with their content unchanged. The order of comment lines does not affect the interpretation of the SAM file.

evidence.quote: Comment lines (CO) in SAM headers are free-form text; their order is not semantically significant.

quarantine.mr_name: HD VN minor version bump preserves compatibility
quarantine.oracle: All header records and all SAM record fields remain identical. Only the VN minor version in the HD line changes, which should not affect parsing of the file content.

evidence.quote: Minor version bumps in the SAM format version indicate backward-compatible changes; the file structure and semantics are preserved.

quarantine.mr_name: Unmapped record field normalization preserves semantics
quarantine.oracle: For unmapped reads (flag bit 0x4 set), the RNAME, POS, MAPQ, CIGAR, RNEXT, PNEXT, and TLEN fields are normalized to standard values (e.g., RNAME='*', POS=0, MAPQ=255, CIGAR='*'). The read name, flag, SEQ, and optional tags remain identical.

evidence.quote: SAM specification states that for unmapped reads, RNAME should be '*' and POS should be 0. Normalizing these fields preserves the semantics of the unmapped record.

quarantine.mr_name: Hard/soft clip CIGAR toggle preserves alignment
quarantine.oracle: The read name, flag, RNAME, POS, MAPQ, RNEXT, PNEXT, TLEN, and all optional tags remain identical. The SEQ length changes appropriately (hard-clipped bases removed from SEQ, soft-clipped bases present in SEQ). The alignment position is unchanged.

evidence.quote: Hard clipping (H) removes bases from SEQ while soft clipping (S) keeps them; converting between H and S preserves the alignment position but changes SEQ length accordingly.

quarantine.mr_name: paired_end_flag_consistency
preconditions: Record is first of pair (flag 0x1 set, 0x40 set)
quarantine.oracle: After round-trip, the first-of-pair flag (0x40) and second-of-pair flag (0x80) remain mutually exclusive and consistent with the read pair orientation

evidence.quote: assertPairValid checks first/second-of-pair flag consistency

quarantine.mr_name: read_negative_strand_invariant
preconditions: Record is paired and both ends are mapped
quarantine.oracle: For a mapped paired-end read, the two ends must have opposite strand flags (0x10 XOR 0x20 = true)

evidence.quote: assertPairValid checks that paired mapped reads have opposite strand flags

quarantine.mr_name: null_record_rejection
quarantine.oracle: After write/read round-trip, the resulting SAMRecord must not be null and must pass assertReadValid sanity checks

evidence.quote: assertReadValid throws IllegalArgumentException if SAMRecord is null

quarantine.mr_name: header_vn_minor_bump_roundtrip
preconditions: Header has HD line with VN tag
quarantine.oracle: After bumping the minor version and round-tripping, the header still parses successfully and all records remain valid

evidence.quote: Header version changes should not break record parsing

quarantine.mr_name: cigar_normalization_invariant
preconditions: Record has CIGAR with match operators (= or X) or zero-length operations
quarantine.oracle: After CIGAR canonicalization and round-trip, the alignment position (POS), RNAME, and sequence remain unchanged

evidence.quote: CIGAR normalization preserves alignment semantics

quarantine.mr_name: optional_tag_permutation_invariant
preconditions: Record has at least two optional tags
quarantine.oracle: After permuting optional tag order and round-tripping, all tag keys and values remain identical

evidence.quote: Optional tag order is not semantically meaningful

quarantine.mr_name: unmapped_record_normalization
quarantine.oracle: After normalizing unmapped fields and round-tripping, the record remains unmapped and the read unmapped flag stays set

evidence.quote: Unmapped records have standardized field values

quarantine.mr_name: header_subtag_shuffle_invariant
preconditions: Header has at least one SQ, RG, or PG record with multiple subtags
quarantine.oracle: After shuffling subtag order within header records and round-tripping, all records still parse and align correctly

evidence.quote: Subtag order within header records is not semantically meaningful
